target: eval_concatenate (Primitive::Concatenate) — pure data movement, no arithmetic

lever.description: Replaced the per-output-element concat loop (which, for every element, scanned operands to select the source tensor and recomputed a row-major flat index via a stride loop) with a block decomposition: for each of `outer` blocks (axes < concat axis) and each operand, copy a contiguous run of `dim[axis] * inner` elements (`inner` = product of axes > concat axis) via extend_from_slice. Produces the same element order, so bit-for-bit identical, but via bulk memcpy with no per-element index math.

benchmarks.method: rch Criterion, same-worker back-to-back; baseline = per-element loop, optimized = block extend_from_slice

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/concat_axis1_3x256x128_f64'

benchmarks.honesty_note: 1.34x on the harder axis-1 case (many small per-row blocks); the op is memory-bound (writes the full ~2.4MB output), so removing per-element index overhead is the available gain. The axis-0 (stacking) case decomposes into a few large contiguous copies and benefits more, but a clean axis-0 figure could not be captured this session due to shared-worker load variance, so only the reliably-measured conservative axis-1 number is claimed. A concat_axis0 bench was added for future profiling.

score.note: Opportunity-matrix score; realized speedup is 1.34x on the conservative case.

isomorphism_proof.data_movement_only: Concatenate copies operand Literals into the output; no arithmetic, so output bits equal the (reordered) input bits regardless of copy granularity.
isomorphism_proof.ordering: Row-major output order is preserved: for each outer index the operands' contiguous blocks are appended in operand order, exactly matching the per-coordinate walk.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Dtype-uniformity, rank, and non-concat-dim validations are unchanged and still run before the copy.
isomorphism_proof.bit_identity_proof: New test concatenate_rank3_middle_axis_matches_naive (3 operands, middle axis of a rank-3 tensor) compares the block-copy output to an independent per-coordinate reference; existing concatenate_1d and the full fj-lax suite (1004/1004) pass. No synthetic golden-bits file: concat is a pure permutation/copy and the parity test pins the element order.
